chunks.text:
Statement: Guidance on qualifying worldwide revenue - online safety fees and penalties
Statement: Guidance on qualifying worldwide revenue - online safety fees and penalties
Online safety
Illegal and harmful content
Consultations and statements
Published: 18 July 2025
Consultation closes: 10 September 2025
Status: Statement Published (Closed)
Last updated:
21 November 2025
Statement published 21 November 2025
Today we are publishing our second statement to implement the online safety fees regime. Given the complementary nature of the Qualifying Worldwide Revenue (QWR) and Notification guidance documents, we have decided to publish a single statement. We have carefully considered responses to our public consultations, which ran from 18 July 2025 to 10 September 2025 for the QWR guidance and 1 September 2025 to 1 October 2025 for the Notification guidance. This statement summarises the outcomes of these consultations, sets out our final positions and includes a final version of both our QWR and Notification guidance in the annexes. We intend to continue our engagement with stakeholders through the implementation of the fees regime including supporting providers in navigating the notification process. The Online Safety Act 2023 (Act) requires that Ofcom’s operating costs for the online safety regime are recovered through fees imposed on certain providers of regulated services. In particular, providers whose QWR is above a threshold set by the Secretary of State (following advice from Ofcom) and which are not exempt from the duty to pay fees. On 26 June 2025, the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology laid
The Online Safety Act 2023 (Qualifying Worldwide Revenue) Regulations 2025
which set out the approach for providers to calculate their QWR. These Regulations define QWR in respect of a provider as the total amount of revenue that arises in connection with the provision of relevant parts of its regulated service(s) anywhere in the world. They also require that providers take a ‘just and reasonable approach’ to calculating QWR when  apportioning revenue that arises partly in connection with the provision of relevant parts of a regulated service and partly in connection with other things.

Main documents Statement Guidance on QWR & Notification • PDF • 740.87 KB • 21 November 2025 Guidance on notification – online safety fees • PDF • 1 MB • 21 November 2025 Guidance on qualifying worldwide revenue – online safety fees and penalties • PDF • 425.01 KB • 21 November 2025 Supporting documents Annex A Definitions and abbreviations • PDF • 308.57 KB • 21 November 2025 Consultation documents Consultation: Guidance on qualifying worldwide revenue.pdf • PDF • 203.6 KB • 18 July 2025 Annex 1: Draft guidance on Qualifying Worldwide Revenue • PDF • 326.66 KB • 18 July 2025 Annexes 2-5: Responding to our consultation • PDF • 181.76 KB • 18 July 2025 Responses Google • PDF • 152.89 KB • 27 October 2025 LinkedIn Corp. • PDF • 72.98 KB • 27 October 2025 Meta Platforms Inc. & WhatsApp LLC • PDF • 255.96 KB • 27 October 2025 Skyscanner • PDF • 177.29 KB • 27 October 2025 Uber Eats • PDF • 85.76 KB • 27 October 2025 UK Interactive Entertainment (Ukie) • PDF • 177.33 KB • 27 October 2025 Vinted • PDF • 157.27 KB • 27 October 2025 Contact information Address Online Safety Fees team Ofcom Riverside House 2A Southwark Bridge Road London SE1 9HA Email : osfeesregime@ofcom.org.uk Consultation: Statement of Charging Principles - Online Safety Fees Ofcom is publishing a consultation on its Statement of Charging Principles (SoCP) for its online safety fees regime. The SoCP will set out the principles we propose to apply when setting fees, and details of the invoicing and payment processes. Online safety fees: what the duties are and how to comply with them Find out about the new fees-related duties imposed on certain providers of regulated services under the Online Safety Act and how to comply with them. Statement: Online Safety - fees and penalties Ofcom is consulting on proposals to implement the fees and penalties regime for online safety, under the Online Safety Act 2023.
